issues.description: All four issues had explicit, well-formed acceptance-criteria sections (e.g. issue #1: `node -e "...DRUMROTS.length"` prints `31`; no entry has tier key `drumrot_god`; `bpm/groove/power` are JS numbers). The orchestrator's §5b mandates `acceptance-contract` before any `coding-agent` dispatch on such issues. It was skipped — no `docs/ai/contracts/issue-N.json` was generated and no failing contract test was written first.
issues.detected_by: Self-review of transcript; orchestrator never announced 'Using acceptance-contract...'.

issues.description: Pre-completion verification was not dispatched. The orchestrator ran ad-hoc `node` import shape checks inline and called that 'verified'. The skill mandates 'Always, before claiming done, opening PR, or running project-state-updater'. PR #5 was opened against `main` before any verification-gate evidence block was emitted.
issues.detected_by: No 'Using verification-gate...' announce string in transcript; no PASS report block before `gh pr create`.

issues.description: Per workflow-orchestrator §6b, `failure-postmortem` must be dispatched after every manual smoke regardless of result. User reported a successful Playwright + computer-control smoke pass (with one favicon-404 finding); no postmortem JSON was written by the run. This retrospective postmortem is the first artifact in `.agent-stack/postmortems/` for this run.
issues.detected_by: Self-review; `ls .agent-stack/postmortems/` returned 'No such file or directory' before this retro began.

issues.description: Memory updates were performed inline by the orchestrator (writing `docs/ai/project-state.md` and `docs/ai/decisions.md`) without dispatching the `project-state-updater` skill or following its Completion Checklist. The post-merge state update almost went directly to `main` (`git push origin main`), caught only by the host's auto-mode classifier — exactly the failure mode an explicit `project-state-updater` invocation should prevent.
issues.detected_by: Tool-call log shows direct `git push origin main` attempt at the post-merge state-update step; denial reason cited AGENTS.md rule violation.

issues.description: Orchestrator entry was correctly announced (the skill *did* invoke at session start). However, after entering, it executed the entire chain in-process without invoking any downstream skill. This is a soft W2: routing through the orchestrator does not, by itself, satisfy the chain — each downstream skill still needs to be entered (subagent or in-process), and that did not happen.
issues.detected_by: Transcript shows only one 'Using workflow-orchestrator...' announce; no subsequent 'Using <skill>...' lines.

failure_output: No functional failure. Outcome was PR #5 merged to main and user-confirmed smoke pass. The 'failure' is purely process: 5 of 6 expected chain skills were skipped because the work felt small enough to do in-process, and the orchestrator skill currently treats subagent dispatch as the only execution mode.
suggested_follow_up: Add an explicit 'in-process execution mode' clause to workflow-orchestrator so 'I judged the work too small for a subagent' does not silently downgrade the chain to 'orchestrator + ad-hoc impl'. Each downstream skill in the chain must be entered (Skill tool, or document the in-process invocation with its announce string + completion checklist) even when no subagent is spawned. Counter-pattern to address: 'tightly-coupled, well-specified issues' → 'just implement directly' → gates dropped.
